mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or the correlation between RSAFS growth and SPX returns turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the market consistently rallies despite high unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and weak housing starts suggests an accommodative monetary policy environment aimed at stimulating the economy, which typically supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns as the economy recovers.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy during housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances where both conditions are met, indicating policy ineffectiveness or deeper economic issues.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth reflects strong manufacturing activity and supply-side expansion, indicating a healthy economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during periods of industrial expansion are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above a certain threshold signals rising costs for businesses and consumers, potentially leading to tighter monetary policy and reduced corporate profitability, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the inflation signal, or the market consistently ignores high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strong and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low GDP and high unemployment indicates a severe economic contraction, leading to significantly reduced corporate earnings and heightened market uncertainty, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both low GDP and high unemployment conditions are met, indicating a market bottom or policy-induced recovery.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals a weakening housing market and broader economic demand, reducing construction activity and consumer confidence, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the weak housing signal, or the market consistently rallies despite a housing downturn.
